The concept of technical debt is borrowed from finance: you take a shortcut now and pay interest later in slower development. In my experience, the teams that ship fastest are not the ones with zero debt, but the ones who track it deliberately. I think the biggest mistake is treating all debt as bad. Some debt is a loan you take on purpose. When I was at my last company, we deliberately shipped a hacky version to learn whether anyone wanted the feature, and that saved us months. So my opinion is: be intentional, write it down, and revisit it.
